clusters.canonicalTopic: Security boundaries, verification gates, and governance before execution
clusters.synthesis: These talks all center on preventing unsafe actions by adding policy checks, proof or verification steps, supply-chain scrutiny, and human accountability before an agent is allowed to act.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Evaluation design and benchmark integrity for agent systems
clusters.synthesis: These candidates focus on how to measure agent behavior without letting the system game the metric, including benchmark hardening, task-specific review, golden sets, closed-loop evaluation, and outcome-driven measurement.
These talks describe memory as a persistent system, using consolidation, librarian-style context, graph memory, and a search-vs-memory split to preserve continuity over time.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Agent-facing web interaction and rendered UI control
clusters.synthesis: These candidates treat the web or rendered UI as the agent substrate, emphasizing delegated control, perception of the rendered interface, and HTML as the execution surface.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Retrieval, context graphs, and navigable knowledge structures
clusters.synthesis: These talks frame retrieval as a structured context layer, combining layered search, mixed-trust facts, context graphs, and auditable paths through navigable information.
These candidates center on explicit concept graphs and formal semantic constraints as the backbone for how an organization models and reasons about its domain.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Sandbox persistence and agent runtime isolation
clusters.synthesis: This candidate is about durable sandbox state that survives failures and long-running work, which is a core sandbox-runtime concern.

These talks describe how coding agents are organized, observed, debugged, and extended in production, including separation of orchestration from reasoning, trace-based debugging, and flexibility in model or harness choice.
These candidates are about moving work off the model, overlapping slow training or rollout phases, and optimizing local or device-constrained inference paths.
This candidate is about the broader product-market shape of software-factory systems and how the category may consolidate over time.
This candidate is about using small interactive worlds as a way to understand a larger system by direct exploration.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Comparative trait analysis of people or minds
clusters.synthesis: This candidate is about comparing notable minds across recurring dimensions to extract patterns rather than describing one person in isolation.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Empty instructions and low-value skill content
clusters.synthesis: This candidate isolates instructions that do not change behavior and should be removed because they add cost without value.
Data derived from real workflows, reasoning traces, and decision sequences rather than only final outputs.
The distinction between what the operator thinks they know and the real constraints in the environment.
Evaluating models in a live but hidden system with limited information and no source access.
The tension between simple out-of-the-box usage and the flexibility of custom local systems.
